title: Events, Held State, Actions, Remapping, Focus, and Devices · Transfer Build
summary: An independent adaptive-arcade team receives a different viewport, device, frame rate, seed, entity count, accessibility profile, platform, and delivery constraint and transfers events, held state, actions, remapping, focus, and devices into a changed device-independent remappable action layer. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of device-independent remappable action layer with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory,…
objectives: Drain or pump the bounded event queue every frame and distinguish ordered events from sampled device state.
Map press, release, repeat, held, analog, and one-shot behavior to named actions with testable transitions.
Store configurable action bindings, detect conflicts, offer alternatives, and avoid unmodified character-key shortcuts where inappropriate.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Event Queue
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Asteroids team handles case 4181 during a low-vision laptop playtest at 125 percent scaling. Build deterministic Python evidence to separate discrete events from held actions while preserving remapping, focus-loss release, and device-independent intent; preserve explicit pixels, seconds, radians or degrees, frames, entity counts, collision units, and budgets; then run the packaged build with dummy video and audio drivers, missing optional sound, and a corrupted save. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Drain or pump the bounded event queue every frame and distinguish ordered events from sampled device state.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Event Queue.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for drain or pump the bounded event queue every frame and distinguish ordered events from sampled device state. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Event Queue, keep Event Queue connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Input Accessibility
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: asteroids-input-accessibility-1l6u7d" that uses case 8318 to separate discrete events from held actions while preserving remapping, focus-loss release, and device-independent intent. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native pygame-ce, SDL, window, controller, mixer, filesystem, package, GPU, installer, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, released pygame-ce 2.5.7, fixed and headless SDL fixtures, real devices, licensed assets, accessibility playtests, profiling, clean installations, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Input Accessibility.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Input Accessibility into observable performance evidence. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Input Accessibility, keep Input Accessibility connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-input-accessibility-1l6u7d
# Competency: asteroids-input-accessibility.
# Case 8318: a low-power integrated-GPU frame-budget trial.
# Operating constraint: keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native display, device, audio, file, package, and operating-system effects.
# Changed case: cross both screen seams in one update and retain collision and rendering continuity.
def evidence_asteroids_input_accessibility_1l6u7d(event_frames: int = 6, pumped_frames: int = 6, actions_bound: int = 5, duplicate_bindings: int = 0, focus_lost_released: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1l6u7d = "1l6u7d-d7u6l1-8318"
    assert evidence_variant_1l6u7d
    evidence_axes_1l6u7d = (
        "player1l6u7d",
        "seed8318",
        "inputd7u6l1",
        "frame1l6u7d8318",
        "state83181l6u7d",
        "playtestd7u6l18318",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1l6u7d)) == 6
    if event_frames < 1 or actions_bound < 3:
        return False, actions_bound, "input-shape"
    if pumped_frames != event_frames:
        return False, pumped_frames, "event-starvation"
    if duplicate_bindings > 0:
        return False, duplicate_bindings, "binding-conflict"
    if not focus_lost_released:
        return False, actions_bound, "stuck-input"
    assert pumped_frames == event_frames
    return True, actions_bound, "remappable-input"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Test an acceptance boundary · Lab Input Accessibility
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Asteroids team handles case 8133 during an offline cross-platform release rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to separate discrete events from held actions while preserving remapping, focus-loss release, and device-independent intent; record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, playtest observation, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then inject a 400 millisecond frame stall and prove the update budget prevents a spiral of death. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Support keyboard and controller, reduced-complexity modes, toggle or hold options, sensitivity, dead zones, timing relief, and pause.

One precondition fails while output still looks plausible. What is the correct response?

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the transferred model · Lab Input Accessibility
steps.instruction:
Write the lab evidence note for Input Accessibility: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A lab Asteroids team handles case 6514 during a keyboard-only community arcade night. Build deterministic Python evidence to separate discrete events from held actions while preserving remapping, focus-loss release, and device-independent intent; record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, playtest observation, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then run the packaged build with dummy video and audio drivers, missing optional sound, and a corrupted save. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Support keyboard and controller, reduced-complexity modes, toggle or hold options, sensitivity, dead zones, timing relief, and pause.

content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Support keyboard and controller, reduced-complexity modes, toggle or hold options, sensitivity, dead zones, timing relief, and pause.
